title: Sour Velvety German Sauerkraut and Beef Sauerkraut Roast
description: A classic German sauerbraten roast, infused with the tanginess of sauerkraut and capers, all while being FODMAP-friendly and cooked sous-vide for a tender, velvety texture.

ingredients.name: 1 cup sauerkraut, drained of liquid and chopped (250g)

instructions: Season the beef with salt and pepper.
In a large bowl, combine the sauerkraut, caraway seeds, garlic, brown sugar, beef broth, apple cider vinegar, red wine vinegar, olive oil, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to combine.
Add the beef to the bowl and toss to coat with the sauerkraut mixture.
Seal the beef in a sous-vide bag.
Cook the beef in a water bath at 130°F (54°C) for 48-72 hours.
Transfer the beef to a roasting pan and spoon the sauerkraut mixture over the top.
Roast the beef in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the top is caramelized.
Remove the beef from the oven and let it rest for 10-15 minutes.
Slice the beef against the grain and serve with the sauerkraut mixture spooned over the top.
Garnish with chopped capers and serve immediately.
